articles.title: QUESTIONS
articles.author: Lulu W. Nelson
articles.complete_text:
Do you ever stop as the days pass by,
To look overhead at the clear blue sky,
Or list to the song of a care-free bird,
Or the rustle of leaves by a light breeze stirred?

Do you catch the hum of the busy bee,
Or watch how the shades steal O'er the lea,
As the cloud-boats glide through the azure sky
And hide the sun as they pass him by?

Do you ever stop when the night hangs low
And see the moon, as a silver bow
Or a radiant orb, with stars awake
And shining below in pool and lake?

Do you ever catch, on the soft spring breeze,
The fragrance shed by the blooming trees,
Or behold a daisy in her bed of sod,
And say to yourself, "There is no God"?
articles.notes: This is a complete poem by Lulu W. Nelson with clear poetic structure and spiritual theme, distinct from the surrounding advertisements and table of contents

misc_summary: The remaining content consists of front matter (table of contents, masthead information), advertisements for various businesses (jewelry, clothing, dairy products, insurance, etc.), and subscription/publication details. This is legitimate miscellaneous content that should not be extracted as standalone articles.
